title: Encoder-Decoder: Sequence Translation
concept: Encoder-decoder models combine both stacks, using cross-attention to translate or summarize input sequences directly.

exercises.prompt: Which model architecture is specifically designed to translate sequences from one language to another by using both input processing and output generation?
exercises.explain: An Encoder-Decoder model combines both processing stacks to handle complex tasks like translation, where the input and output are sequences of varying lengths.

cards.title: From One Language to Another
cards.body: How do AI systems translate entire paragraphs from French to Japanese without losing the meaning? They don't just swap words out one by one. Instead, they use a double-team architecture to understand the whole sentence before rewriting it!

cards.title: What is Encoder-Decoder?
cards.body: An Encoder-Decoder model is a neural network architecture that combines two main parts. The encoder processes an input sequence (like a sentence in Spanish) into a dense representation. The decoder then takes this representation and generates a new output sequence (like the translated sentence in English), utilizing cross-attention to focus on the right input parts at each step.

cards.title: The Conference Interpreter
cards.body: Think of a professional human interpreter at a global summit. First, they listen to a speech in French, writing down the core ideas in their notepad (Encoding). Once the speaker finishes, the interpreter reads their notes and speaks the translation aloud in English (Decoding). They don't just translate word-for-word; they look at the whole message!

cards.title: Translation in Action
cards.body: When you type 'How are you?' into a modern translation app and get '¿Cómo estás?', an Encoder-Decoder model is working behind the scenes. The encoder reads the entire English sentence, and the decoder generates the Spanish equivalent word by word, checking back with the encoder's notes to ensure accuracy.

cards.title: Not Just a Dictionary
cards.body: Many people think translation AI works by looking up words in a dictionary and pasting them together. In reality, word-for-word translation creates broken sentences. Encoder-decoder models translate concepts and context, ensuring the output flows naturally in the new language.

cards.title: Encoder vs. Decoder
cards.body: Let's look at how the workload is split between the two systems to translate text.

right.text: Reads the map of meaning and generates the new sentence word-by-word in the target language.

left.text: Reads the input sentence, analyzes the grammar, and creates a map of the meaning.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary job of the 'Encoder' part of the model?

options.text: It prints the final translated words to the screen.
It processes the input sequence into a meaningful summary representation.
It randomly shuffles the words in the sentence.
It deletes the input to save computer memory.

exercises.explain: The encoder's role is to analyze and compress the entire input text into a summary representation so the decoder can work with it.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Encoder-decoder models can only be used for word translation and nothing else.
exercises.explain: Encoder-decoder models are highly flexible and are used for translating, summarizing text, rewriting, and conversational responses.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing term that connects the two stacks in sequence-to-sequence tasks:
exercises.sentence: The decoder connects to the encoder using a mechanism called [blank] to look back at the source text.
exercises.explain: Cross-attention acts as the bridge, allowing the decoder to look back at the encoder's representation of the input sequence.

exercises.prompt: Which of these systems is using an Encoder-Decoder model for sequence translation?

options.text: System A: An AI reads an entire English paragraph, processes the meaning, and then writes it out in French.
System B: A software program swaps every word in a sentence with its direct dictionary translation.
System C: A model takes a text prompt and generates a matching photo.

exercises.explain: System A represents a standard Encoder-Decoder translating a full sequence, while System B describes a simple dictionary lookup. System C describes an image generator.

exercises.prompt: In a sequence-to-sequence model, why is cross-attention so important for the decoder?

options.text: It connects the decoder to the encoder's output so it can refer back to the original message.
It blocks the decoder from seeing what it has already generated.
It deletes all the punctuation and spaces from the translation.
It forces the model to only translate one single word at a time without context.

exercises.explain: Encoder-decoder models use cross-attention to look back at the source sequence (represented by the encoder) while generating the target sequence step-by-step (with the decoder).
